Title: The American Labor Movement: A Short History
Creator: U.S. Department of Labor
Description: A historical overview of the American labor movement published by the U.S. Department of Labor covering major milestones from the 19th century through the mid-20th century.
Subject: labor history; unions; workers rights; American history

Title: Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee (SNCC) Records
Creator: Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee
Description: Digitized records and publications from the SNCC including newsletters, meeting minutes, and organizational documents from the civil rights era.
Subject: civil rights; student activism; SNCC; social movements; university activism

Title: Industrial Workers of the World: Proceedings of the First Convention
Creator: Industrial Workers of the World
Description: Full proceedings of the founding convention of the Industrial Workers of the World held in Chicago in June 1905, including speeches and founding documents.
Subject: labor unions; IWW; Wobblies; labor organizing; workers rights

Title: Free Speech Movement: UC Berkeley 1964
Creator: Bancroft Library — UC Berkeley
Description: Collection of documents, photographs, and primary sources related to the Free Speech Movement at UC Berkeley in 1964, one of the landmark student activism events in American history.
Subject: student activism; free speech; UC Berkeley; university protests; 1960s

Title: Women in Industry: A Study in American Economic History
Creator: Edith Abbott
Description: A comprehensive early study on the role of women in American industry and the labor force, documenting working conditions and wages in the early 20th century.
Subject: women; labor; industry; economic history; gender and work
